info.title: Wayfront API
info.description: With Wayfront, how you sell and deliver services is no longer the bottleneck. We unify your process, automate and remove steps, and help clients find what they need.

get.description: Return a list of Coupon objects. The coupons are sorted by creation date with the most recent coupons appearing first. You can filter and sort the results using query parameters.

parameters.name: filters[id][$in][]
parameters.description: Example filter on user_id. You can filter by any other field or operator and combine multiple filters. Supported operators include $eq (=), $lt (<), $gt (>), $in (in array, requires an array of values).

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

get.description: Retrieves a Coupon object.

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

put.description:
Updates the specified coupon by setting the values of the parameters passed. Any parameters not provided will be left unchanged.
        
This request accepts mostly the same arguments as the coupon creation call.

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

delete.description: Deletes a coupon. It can be undone.

get.description: Return a list of FilledFormField objects for a specific order or ticket. Either `order` or `ticket` parameter must be provided, but not both.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Order ID, if provided will return only filled form fields for this order, can't be used with ticket parameter

parameters.name: ticket
parameters.description: Ticket ID, if provided will return only filled form fields for this ticket, can't be used with order parameter

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

put.description: Updates the specified filled form field by setting the values of the parameters passed. Any parameters not provided will be left unchanged.

parameters.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

delete.description: Permanently deletes a filled form field. It cannot be undone.

post.description: Charges an invoice using a payment method ID. If the invoice has a custom billing date, it will be removed.

get.description: Return a list of Invoice objects. The invoices are sorted by creation date in descending order. You can filter by user_id, status, and other fields. You can also sort by any field.

parameters.name: filters[user_id][$in][]
parameters.description: Example filter on user_id. You can filter by any other field or operator and combine multiple filters. Supported operators include $eq (=), $lt (<), $gt (>), $in (in array, requires an array of values).

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

get.description: Retrieves an Invoice object.

put.description: Updates the specified invoice by setting the values of the parameters passed. Any parameters not provided will be left unchanged.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an invoice. This action cannot be undone.

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

post.description: Mark invoice as paid. If the invoice is already paid, an error will be thrown. It will be marked as paid with the payment method "Manual". Only invoices that have user assigned can be marked as paid. In case of recurring invoices, it will create subscription for the user if it does not exist. It will also create corresponding orders for the invoice items.

get.description: Return a list of Log objects. The logs are sorted by creation date in descending order.

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

get.description: Generates a magic link for the specified user. The user must be client user, not staff. Users that have 2FA enabled cannot use magic links. Magic link is valid for 2 hour. If the user has a previously generated magic link, it will be reused.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The client user to generate a magic link for.

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

get.description: Return a list of IndexOrder objects. The orders are sorted by creation date in descending order. Keep in mind that IndexOrder is a simplified version of the Order object, which contains only the most essential fields. If you need more details about an order, use the Retrieve an order endpoint.

parameters.name: filters[user_id][$in][]
parameters.description: Example filter on user_id. You can filter by any other field or operator and combine multiple filters. Supported operators include $eq (=), $lt (<), $gt (>), $in (in array, requires an array of values).

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description: A response containing an error message.

NotFoundErrorResponse.title: NotFoundErrorResponse
NotFoundErrorResponse.description: A response containing an error message.

message.example: No query results for model [App\Models\{Model}] E4A269FC

UnauthenticatedErrorResponse.title: UnauthenticatedErrorResponse
UnauthenticatedErrorResponse.description: A response containing an error message.

Size.description: Key-value pair of service pricing option

Color.description: Key-value pair of service pricing option

content.example: Client called to discuss project requirements.

Size.description: Key-value pair of service pricing option

Color.description: Key-value pair of service pricing option

team.description: Team Permission. Same as settings_team for backward compatibility.

settings.description: Settings Permission, this only indicates if the role has access to settings or not, for more granular permissions check other fields. Kept for backward compatibility.

description.example: Create a new logo for the client

is_public.description: Indicates whether the task is visible on the portal side.

for_client.description: If true, clients are allowed to complete this task.

deadline.description: Number of hours until deadline from previous task or order creation. For days use 24-hour increments. Mutually exclusive with `due_at`.

due_at.description: Due at, specific date and time the task is due. Mutually exclusive with `deadline`.

OrderOrTicketRequiredErrorResponse.description: You must provide either order or ticket parameter.

message.example: You must provide either order or ticket parameter.

X-Api-Identifier.description: Unique identifier for the request, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.description: API version, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

X-Api-Version.name: X-Api-Version
X-Api-Version.description: API version identifier, in case of any issues with API please provide this identifier when contacting support

order.description: Order number REQUIRED, can't be used with ticket parameter

ticket.description: Ticket number REQUIRED, can't be used with order parameter

employee_ids.description: Employee IDs assigned to the task

description.example: Create a new logo for the client

is_public.description: Indicates whether the task is visible on the portal side.

for_client.description: If true, clients are allowed to complete this task. Mutually exclusive with employee_ids.

deadline.description: Deadline in hours. For days use 24-hour increments. Mutually exclusive with `due_at`.

service.description: Order title (required unless service_id is provided)

recurring.description: Recurring; 0 - one time, 1 - recurring, 2 - trial or setup fee
